pattern: Sorting turns 'how many pairs beat a threshold' into a converging two-pointer sweep: fix the small pointer and every index between it and the shrinking large pointer is a free win.
hints: Once the array is sorted, fixing the smaller of two indices lets you reason about a whole contiguous range of partners at once.

With lo at the small end and hi at the large end, if nums[lo] + nums[hi] is already under target, every index strictly between lo and hi paired with lo also works - count them all at once instead of one at a time.
Sort nums. Walk lo from 0 and hi from n-1 inward: while lo < hi, if nums[lo] + nums[hi] < target, add (hi - lo) to the count and advance lo; otherwise pull hi in by one.

edge_inputs.description: two elements that do not qualify
all identical values, some pairs equal target exactly
minimum and maximum magnitude values mixed
target below every possible pair sum yields zero
target above every possible pair sum counts them all
duplicates must each be counted as distinct index pairs, not deduplicated by value
unsorted input with a strict-inequality boundary case

stress.description: 50 random values near the constraint edges, mixing positive and negative
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(-50, 51) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randrange(-50, 51)
    return [nums, target]
stress.note: n is capped at the constraint max (50), so this exercises the full-size case deterministically
